title: Nature of Seed Carriers Moons
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 18, 2011 9:58 am thewayneiac wrote: No, the Black Moon arrived on a spaceship.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:02 pm NemZ wrote: And you're getting this idea from where, exactly? I smell an ass-pull.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:04 pm For whatever it's worth, my reading of that is the "moons" (spaceships) were the Black/White Moons themselves. But maybe that wouldn't make any sense considering--in EoE at least--the size of the Black Moon compared to Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:10 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: For whatever it's worth, my reading of that is the "moons" (spaceships) were the Black/White Moons themselves. But maybe that wouldn't make any sense considering--in EoE at least--the size of the Black Moon compared to Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:14 pm Who says Lilith was always GNR-sized? Adam sure didn't seem to be so large when the White Moon was found - and if I recall, Adam was found inside it. Come to think of it, wasn't Lilith found inside the Black Moon as well? Where and how did they find Lilith? I'm actually quite baffled by that, now that I think about it. ETA: Where's the White Moon's carrier? lol backstory

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:30 pm Azathoth wrote: Who says Lilith was always GNR-sized? Adam sure didn't seem to be so large when the White Moon was found - and if I recall, Adam was found inside it. Come to think of it, wasn't Lilith found inside the Black Moon as well? Where and how did they find Lilith? I'm actually quite baffled by that, now that I think about it. ETA: Where's the White Moon's carrier? lol backstory

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:32 pm Hmm, crazy. Oh well, one more stupid thing to put on my "ignore this bullshit from the CI" pile.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:36 pm thewayneiac wrote: Lilith was placed in the Black Moon, which was transported to Earth onboard a carrier ship known as a moon.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:45 pm Allemann wrote: That doesn't make the Moon a spaceship. It's an artificial vessel.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:58 pm I figured the outer "carrier" was just some kind of protective planetoid, and the inner part contained whatever mysterious mechanism actually propelled the thing... ATF-based, maybe. Then again... :muses: thewayneiac wrote: The Dead Sea Scrolls probably revealed the location of both Adam and Lilith, who were deposited on Earth inside the White and Black Moons, by their carrier moons.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Fri Mar 18, 2011 3:41 pm In turn raising obnoxious questions like how the Dead Sea Scrolls got wherever Seele found them...

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Fri Mar 18, 2011 3:51 pm NemZ wrote: Hmm, crazy. Oh well, one more stupid thing to put on my "ignore this bullshit from the CI" pile.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Mar 18, 2011 3:58 pm rhfxz1s wrote: I don't really have any sources to cite with this, but I was under the impression that the Dead Sea Scrolls were found inside either the Black Moon or the White Moon (or both). This would make the most sense, since they came from the FAR along with the Seeds of Life. Why it would the FAR want to put them anywhere else?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 18, 2011 4:09 pm thewayneiac wrote: @ Reichu, I'm not certain if the "melting Moon" theory is simpler. It makes at least as much sense to say that things worked properly with Adam. Adam was supposed to be here. His carrier isn't here any more. Thus his carrier wasn't supposed to stick around.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 18, 2011 5:07 pm COACH wrote: How's it bullshit? Do you have a better idea of how it all went down?

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Fri Mar 18, 2011 5:32 pm Perhaps all this "moon inside a moon" stuff is a translation quirk. I really need to learn Japanese!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 18, 2011 6:31 pm NemZ wrote: Can't seeds keep themselves perfectly safe with their ATFs without the need for any such protective stuff, regardless?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 18, 2011 7:01 pm My point is that THE moon should not be the same as this carrier moon thingamajig, regardless. And it's still silly. Why can't the geofront moon things just be the only crazy alien earthenware spaceships involved, without all this other rigmarole? Stupid CI nonsense...

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Fri Mar 18, 2011 7:23 pm NemZ wrote: My point is that THE moon should not be the same as this carrier moon thingamajig, regardless. And it's still silly. Why can't the geofront moon things just be the only crazy alien earthenware spaceships involved, without all this other rigmarole? Stupid CI nonsense...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 18, 2011 7:29 pm @rhfxz: First, I think you're getting the two moons confused. (Lilith's is the one that went ker-pow.) Second, the artificial "cavity" part really isn't that big; just 6km across. The Giant Impact theory calls for a Mars-sized planetoid, doesn't it? NemZ wrote: My point is that THE moon should not be the same as this carrier moon thingamajig, regardless.
summary:
[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 18, 2011 9:58 am thewayneiac wrote: No, the Black Moon arrived on a spaceship.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:02 pm NemZ wrote: And you're getting this idea from where, exactly? I smell an ass-pull.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » F
